steps.text: First — I'm big and spiky on the outside, yellow and sweet inside. I grow in hot countries like Brazil. Find me!
steps.feedback: Pineapple! They grow in tropical countries near the equator.

steps.text: I'm round and hard with a hairy brown shell. Inside I'm white with sweet water. I grow on tall palm trees near the sea.
steps.feedback: Coconut! Coconuts grow on beaches in places like India and Sri Lanka.

steps.text: This one is green or yellow outside and bright orange inside — very sweet and juicy. What colour do you think it is inside? Find it!
steps.feedback: Mango! Orange inside! Mangoes come from India and Africa.

steps.text: Find the small oval one — green and fuzzy. Its name starts with the /k/ sound, just like coconut. Kk... find it!
steps.feedback: Kiwi! /k/ like coconut — same starting sound. Kiwi comes from New Zealand.

steps.text: Last two — find the pear-shaped green one that's creamy inside and goes on toast, and the big pale one with soft orange flesh.
steps.feedback: Avocado and melon — all six exotic fruits found! They came from Brazil, India, New Zealand, and beyond.
